name: todo-cli
summary: Greenfield: build a usable, persistent command-line todo application from a one-line brief.

rubric.guidance: From a fresh checkout a reviewer can add, list, complete, and delete tasks and see correct results each time. Partial or stubbed commands score low.

rubric.criterion: State persists across invocations and is robust
rubric.guidance: Tasks survive process exit (a file or db). Normal use does not corrupt state; reading an empty/new store does not crash.

rubric.criterion: Tests meaningfully cover behavior and pass
rubric.guidance: Tests drive real command behavior (not trivial asserts) and would catch a regression in add/list/complete/delete. They are green.
Clear --help, sensible errors on bad input or unknown ids, no stack traces leaking to the user.
Readable, cohesive code; no gold-plating or unrelated churn; the change history (commits/PRs) tells a coherent story tied to the goal.
